The “prove value” part of the UnitPay tagline is the piece I’m most curious about. Is that meant to connect usage-based billing to customer-facing reports, or more for internal teams to understand which AI features are driving revenue? For AI agents and workflow automation tools, the unit of value can be pretty different from app to app, so I’d be interested in how flexible that model is.

results.title: About UnitPay — Monetization Infrastructure for AI Companies | UnitPay
results.content:
We were founded with the belief that AI companies deserve billing infrastructure
as intelligent as their products. So beyond collecting payment, UnitPay tracks
margins per customer and reads revenue at a granular level, giving teams a clear
picture of what they earn and where it comes from.

## Principles behind the product.

### AI-native by default

Usage is the unit of value for AI products. We meter every event so your billing reflects exactly what customers consume.

### Ship pricing in minutes

Build usage, credit, seat, or hybrid models without writing billing code. Change pricing without waiting on an engineering sprint.

### Prove value to customers

Give teams clear, customer-facing dashboards that show the value delivered, so renewals start from trust instead of confusion. [...] UnitPay

# Billing infrastructure as intelligent as your products.

UnitPay is the monetization and revenue infrastructure built for AI-native
companies. It helps teams meter usage, build any pricing model, track margins,
and understand revenue at a granular level.

## Built for the way AI companies actually grow.

AI products meter value in tokens, runs, and outcomes, not in flat monthly
seats. Legacy billing tools were never built for that. UnitPay starts from the
usage event and turns it into pricing, invoices, and revenue you can reason about.

That means teams can launch any pricing model: usage-based, credits, outcomes,
or a hybrid of all three. As the product evolves, pricing can evolve with it,
without a billing rewrite each time. [...] ### Transparent by design

Track margins per customer and read revenue at a granular level. No black boxes, just an honest view of what you earn.

## Monetize your AI product with confidence.

Start free, or walk through your pricing with us.

results.title: AI Pricing Based on Assumptions, Not Data | Ben Jackson posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content: “AI pricing without real cost data is just guessing.” That was Wade Brogdon, CEO of Unitpay, on this week’s episode of the (AI) People podcast. Unitpay operates in the AI FinOps and usage-based billing infrastructure layer of the AI tech stack. Wade explained that most AI products are priced without any real event-level cost signals. Teams assume they know their unit economics, but the moment usage scales, everything breaks because the underlying data was never connected in the first place. Unitpay’s insight is that reliable AI pricing isn’t a spreadsheet exercise. It requires a direct feed from production workloads so models, agents and pipelines are costed accurately. 👉 How many AI teams do you think are still pricing their products based on assumptions rather than real data?
